title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old driver died in Marlborough, NH, after his pickup truck hit a school bus carrying 13 students.
The crash occurred on Route 12; the students from Monadnock Regional Middle High School reported no serious injuries.
The collision is being investigated by the NH State Police, Marlborough police, and fire officials.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning, securing a 3-2 win for the Braves over the Red Sox.
The Red Sox fell to a season record of 18-26 after their defeat at the hands of the Braves.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs over five innings, striking out six and allowing no walks.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs and the New England Patriots have mutual interest, but a reunion is unlikely due to A.J. Brown's trade.
Patriots are set to acquire A.J. Brown, impacting salary cap space, limiting possibilities of adding Stefon Diggs as a third receiver.
Diggs had a 1,000-yard season last year with Patriots, but financial constraints and team dynamics hinder his return.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday afternoon near 965 Blue Hill Avenue, Dorchester, Boston police report.
Police describe the missing boy as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, brown eyes, black hair, frequenting local areas.
Boston police urge public to report sightings to 911 or District B-3 detectives; anonymous tips through CrimeStoppers.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man on May 10 in Dorchester.
The 42-year-old woman suffers life-threatening injuries, while the 42-year-old man has non-life-threatening injuries.
Broomes faces charges including assault with intent to murder; a dangerousness hearing is scheduled for May 21.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox starting pitchers have a combined 2.74 ERA and 1.08 WHIP in 39 starts in 2026.
Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and Kyle Schwarber are key former Red Sox still performing well on other teams.
Chris Sale, now with Atlanta, experienced a career resurgence, contributing to questions about Boston's trade decisions.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony of the Red Sox is recovering from a right wrist sprain, no longer wearing a wrist brace.
Anthony played catch but hasn't resumed swinging a bat; he hopes to start soon based on his recovery progress.
Anthony's injury occurred on May 4 against the Tigers, hitting .385 in May before his injury halted the momentum.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally drove into BrightPath daycare in Norwood, Massachusetts, causing moderate structural damage.
The driver, while dropping off a passenger, mistakenly accelerated instead of stopping the moving vehicle.
No injuries were reported, and children inside were safely evacuated; the daycare is temporarily closed for assessment.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict a 10-7 record for the New England Patriots in the 2026 NFL season.
The Patriots face a challenging schedule, including games against Seattle, Denver, and Kansas City.
Sports Illustrated predicts a 9-8 season, citing the team's difficult schedule and offseason challenges.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Marty, Life is Short' featuring Martin Short is now streaming on Netflix, directed by Lawrence Kasdan.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' Season 2 hosted by Colin Jost moves to Netflix with teams reduced to duos.
'Welcome to Wrexham' Season 5 streams on Hulu, continuing Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney's football club journey.
